coin_profile.name: Quant
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: Quant (QNT) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #66 by market cap.

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

moat.rationale: QNT possesses a moderate moat anchored by Quant Network's proprietary Overledger technology, which creates switching costs for enterprise users integrating multi-chain interoperability infrastructure; however, this moat is partially offset by weak network effects (score: 2), unproven token value capture mechanisms, and the reality that the core interoperability problem can theoretically be solved by competitors or layer-1 upgrades. The perfect dilution control (10/10) and reasonable maturity (4/10) provide structural stability, but fee growth and holder value accrual both score only 5/10, indicating the protocol has not yet demonstrated compelling economic moat through cash flows to token holders or strong demand-side network effects typical of tier-1 protocols.

chart_zones.mos_note: Margin of safety present — price near or below speculative fair value during accumulation (bear market) phase.

2026.net_issuance_note: Current snapshot only. Historical supply data requires on-chain indexing not available via free APIs.

meta.data_note: No protocol fee or TVL data found for this token on DeFiLlama. Charts 1 and 2 are unavailable. Charts 3 and 4 show current-year supply and dilution data from CoinGecko.

forecast_meta.growth_basis_note: Annual growth rates derived from quality score 5.3/10 and survivability 63.3%. Base: 7.0% p.a., Bull: 13.0% p.a., Bear: -21.1% p.a.
forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (7.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (5.3/10). Bull (13.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-21.1%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 63.3% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment did not fire: FDV/MC is within the acceptable range for this quality tier, so no dilution penalty was applied. Only 2 methods contributed to the median. More data sources (DeFiLlama fee history) would improve confidence in this estimate. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.

watchlist_signal.reason: Quality Cash-Flow Protocol with a margin of safety present — price near speculative fair value during a constructive cycle phase.

metric_assessments.capital_efficiency: Capital Efficiency is middling. The numbers are acceptable but leave room for improvement.

quality_narrative.classification_reason: Quant is rated Speculative because while it has genuine strengths in dilution control, it also has a notable weakness in network demand that introduces meaningful uncertainty. Speculative means real potential but less predictable outcomes than a Safe-rated asset.
quality_narrative.investment_narrative: Quant is a Cash-Flow Protocol with mixed fundamentals, scoring 5.3/10 overall. Its clearest strength is dilution control, while network demand remains the main area to watch. Key risks: low network demand score (2/10). Quant is appropriate for investors with higher risk tolerance and conviction in its long-term growth thesis.
quality_narrative.quality_conclusion: Quant scores 5.3/10 (Speculative) as a Cash-Flow Protocol. The overall quality profile reflects the protocol's maturity, fee economics, and token supply dynamics relative to peers.

coin_profile.description: Qtum (QTUM) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #299 by market cap.

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

moat.rationale: QTUM has minimal competitive defensibility as a forked Bitcoin/Ethereum hybrid with no unique technical moat; its network effects are weak due to low adoption (rank 299, $0.1B market cap) and limited developer ecosystem lock-in. The protocol captures minimal token value (holder_value_accrual=5) with weak network demand signals (6/10), and despite strong dilution control (10/10), this cannot offset the absence of meaningful switching costs, regulatory advantages, or product differentiation that would justify a higher moat score.

coin_profile.description: Quantum Resistant Ledger (QRL) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #324 by market cap.

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

moat.rationale: QRL (Quantum Resistant Ledger) has a niche technical moat around post-quantum cryptography, but faces severe fundamental weaknesses: minimal network demand (score: 1), extremely low protocol maturity (score: 1), and poor holder value accrual (score: 5) indicate it hasn't achieved product-market fit despite years of development. The protocol is easily forked, has negligible ecosystem lock-in, lacks meaningful network effects, and operates in a speculative space where quantum threats remain theoretical; without demonstrable adoption or fee-generating activity, it functions more as a venture-stage bet than an entrenched protocol with sustainable defensibility.

coin_profile.name: Quantix Finance
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: Quantix Finance (QFI) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #431 by market cap.

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

moat.rationale: QFI exhibits minimal protocol moat characteristics: extremely poor network demand signals (1/10) and virtually no dilution control or maturity (1/10 each) indicate a nascent, struggling protocol with limited adoption and weak tokenomics. The protocol lacks meaningful competitive defensibility, network effects, or ecosystem lock-in typical of defensible cash-flow protocols; at 0.1B market cap with only 10% circulating supply, it faces significant dilution risk and shows no clear evidence of sustainable value capture for token holders.

chart_zones.mos_note: Price above speculative fair value — no margin of safety.

2026.net_issuance_note: Current snapshot only. Historical supply data requires on-chain indexing not available via free APIs.

meta.data_note: No protocol fee or TVL data found for this token on DeFiLlama. Charts 1 and 2 are unavailable. Charts 3 and 4 show current-year supply and dilution data from CoinGecko.

forecast_meta.growth_basis_note: Annual growth rates derived from quality score 2.9/10 and survivability 23.3%. Base: 0.0% p.a., Bull: 6.0% p.a., Bear: -65.6% p.a.
forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (0.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (2.9/10). Bull (6.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-65.6%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 23.3% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Network Activity (NVT) did not contribute to the median: the implied fair value fell outside the 0.1x–10x price bounds (volume is very low or very high relative to circulating supply). Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment penalises the large gap between circulating and fully-diluted supply — future token unlocks are expected to create selling pressure. Only 2 methods contributed to the median. More data sources (DeFiLlama fee history) would improve confidence in this estimate. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.
forecast_meta.price_history_note: Historical price data unavailable for this token. Forecast chart shows projected values only.
